issues.title: Missing Error Handling in Ingestion Command
issues.details: The ingestion command lacks comprehensive error handling, particularly in the upsert process. This could lead to data loss or corruption if an error occurs during batch processing. Suggested fix: Implement try-catch blocks around the upsert logic to handle exceptions gracefully. File: .claude/commands/ingest.md:3-5.

issues.title: Potential Path Traversal Vulnerability
issues.details: The command allows user-specified paths for ingestion without validation, which could lead to path traversal attacks. Suggested fix: Validate and sanitize input paths to ensure they do not escape the intended directory structure. File: .claude/commands/ingest.md:1-2.

issues.title: Lack of Unit Tests for New Logic
issues.details: The ingestion command lacks unit tests for the new logic introduced in the recent changes. This could lead to undetected bugs in production. Suggested fix: Add unit tests covering various scenarios for the ingestion process. File: .claude/commands/ingest.md.

issues.title: Inconsistent Commit Message Format
issues.details: The commit message format in the feature request command does not consistently follow the specified guidelines. Suggested fix: Ensure all commit messages adhere to the defined format. File: .claude/commands/features.md.

summary: The overall code quality shows several areas for improvement, particularly in error handling, security, and testing. Addressing these issues is crucial for maintaining a robust and secure application.
